title: muehlenbeckia nummularia
intro: Muehlenbeckia nummularia, also known as the Creeping Wire Vine or Maidenhair Vine, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, Australia, and parts of the Pacific Islands. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Muehlenbeckia nummularia include its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of Muehlenbeckia nummularia can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
